author: communityMember-a1
text: Discover Weekly keeps recommending songs I already saved months ago. How do I get it to actually find NEW artists instead of recycling my own library back to me?

author: communityMember-b2
text: Is there a way to reset my taste profile? My recommendations got stuck in one genre after a single playlist and now everything Spotify suggests is the same five artists on repeat.

author: communityMember-c3
text: Feature request: a 'discovery mode' slider so I can tell the algorithm how adventurous to be. Some weeks I want safe picks, some weeks I want a real stretch into new music.

author: communityMember-d4
text: Why does Release Radar only show artists I already follow? The whole point was to discover new releases but it never suggests anyone outside my rotation.

author: communityMember-e5
text: I'm trying to get into jazz but every recommendation throws me into the deep end. I bounce off it every time. I wish there were a guided on-ramp that eased me in over a few weeks.

author: communityMember-f6
text: The algorithm feels like an echo chamber. I listen to the same playlist on repeat because nothing it suggests ever sticks, and I have no energy to go crate-digging myself.

author: communityMember-g7
text: Daylist and mood playlists are great but discovery there is accidental — I never actually adopt the new artists, they vanish by the next week. How do people make new music stick?

author: communityMember-h8
text: Power user here: Spotify's recommendations are too safe and mainstream for me. I want deeper, riskier suggestions that still connect to my taste, not the same popular picks everyone gets.
